final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing handscroll painting of the Lantern Festival night panorama in Tang Dynasty Chang'an, rendered in the meticulous gongbi heavy-color (工笔重彩) long-scroll tradition.

Style/medium: gongbi heavy-color (工笔重彩) horizontal handscroll, ink outlines of silk-thread fineness filled with mineral pigments — malachite, azurite, vermilion, and gold leaf — the surface carrying the cool luminous weight of Tang court painting
Composition: sweeping horizontal panorama in the handscroll convention, the eye traveling from gate tower to market street to palace forecourt in a continuous unfolding rhythm, ten thousand lanterns distributed as repeating echoes across the full width
Subject: the Lantern Festival night scene of Tang Chang'an — crowds in Tang-dynasty robes, lantern towers (灯楼) rising above the avenues, palace gates hung with silk lanterns, vendors and performers woven through the throng, the city alive with human warmth
Scene/backdrop: Chang'an's Zhuque Avenue and surrounding wards at the height of the Lantern Festival night, city walls and gate towers receding into the luminous dark
Lighting/mood: ten thousand flower lanterns (万盏花灯) illuminating the night sky — a warm honey glow radiating outward from each lantern cluster, the darkness not extinguished but held in reverent conversation with the light, the mood one of sacred communal joy
Color palette: warm amber and vermilion lantern-light against deep indigo night, punctuated by malachite green rooftiles and gold architectural accents, the chromatic temperature of a city on fire with celebration
Materials/textures: the silken surface of a Tang handscroll — mineral pigments with the dense, matte luminosity of azurite and malachite, gold-leaf lantern halos, fine ink-line architecture with the precision of a court draftsman
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: horizontal long-scroll (长卷) format, gongbi line work throughout, no Western perspective — use traditional Chinese layered-plane spatial convention
Avoid: ink-wash (水墨) looseness, impressionistic brushwork, modern lighting effects, Western oil-painting depth cues
